description: Following the 2008 collapse of Landsbanki, the Icelandic public voted on whether to repay £3.5 billion to the UK and Dutch governments for the 'Icesave' bank accounts. In April 2011, a decisive 59.7% of voters rejected the bailout agreement.

key_events.description: President Grímsson refuses to sign the first Icesave bill, triggering national debate.
International rating agencies threaten Iceland with further downgrades if rejection occurs.
UK and Dutch governments issue final warnings regarding legal consequences.

calibration_notes: Useful for measuring nationalistic sentiment vs. international financial pressure in a post-sovereign default context.
